candidates.text:
"Dear Darius,

I’m writing to apply for the job in Ventura because I heard the coffee there is 30% less likely to cause existential dread than the coffee in my current city. Also, I’m convinced the ocean breeze has been personally assigned to me by the universe, and I’d be a fool to ignore it.

I bring to the table a unique blend of skills: I can organize a spreadsheet while humming a 1980s power ballad, I once successfully negotiated a truce between two arguing seagulls, and I can make a convincing argument for why a sandwich is a valid meal at 2 AM. I’m also fluent in 'please,' 'thank you,' and 'I’ll just try this one more time.'

Ventura sounds like the perfect place to put those skills to use, and I’m ready to dive in—metaphorically and, if necessary, literally. I’d love to discuss how my ability to find a parking spot in a crowded lot could benefit your team.

Looking forward to hearing from you, Darius. Don’t worry, I’ll keep the seagulls in line.

Best,  
[Your Name]"

"Dear Darius,

I am writing to apply for the open position in Ventura. I have decided to pursue this role because I have a unique set of skills that I believe will be an asset to your team. For instance, I can make a perfect cup of coffee every time, and I can also identify a cat's favorite toy from a distance of 50 feet. 

I am confident that my skills will make me an ideal candidate for this position, and I would be thrilled to bring my talents to your team. I have attached my resume and a photo of me holding a cat toy, which I believe will demonstrate my commitment to this role. 

I look forward to hearing from you soon, and I hope that my application will stand out among the other candidates. Thank you for considering me for this opportunity. 

Sincerely, 
Your Name"

"Dear Darius,

I’m writing to apply for the open position in Ventura, and I’d like to start by saying that I’m not just looking for a job—I’m looking for a reason to justify my obsession with the local surf culture, my ability to make a perfect avocado toast, and my uncanny knack for predicting when a seagull is about to steal someone’s sandwich.

I’ve heard Ventura has the best coffee in Southern California (and by “best,” I mean “the kind that makes you feel like you could solve a differential equation while standing in line”). I also understand that you value creativity, reliability, and a healthy dose of humor—so I’ve attached my resume, my list of three reasons why I should be hired (one of which involves a spontaneous ukulele cover of “Sweet Child O’ Mine” during a team meeting), and a photo of me successfully balancing a coconut on my head while walking down a beach.

If you’re looking for someone who can handle the occasional wave of chaos, a sudden urge to chase a dog named “Buster,” and the occasional need for a motivational speech delivered in the voice of a 1970s disco singer, then I’m your guy.

I’d love to chat soon—maybe over a cold brew and a view of the ocean. No pressure, but if you say yes, I’ll bring a playlist of songs that make people want to dance in the office.

Warmly,  
Your Future Colleague (Who Will Definitely Not Steal Your Lunch Again)"

"Dear Darius,

I’m writing to apply for the job in Ventura, and I’d like to start by saying I’ve already mentally moved here. My plant, my cat, and my existential dread are all packed and ready to hit the 101.

I bring to the role a unique blend of skills: I can organize spreadsheets, brew a mean pour-over, and accurately guess when a coworker is about to say something awkward. I once convinced a barista that my ‘extra shot’ request was a medical necessity, and I’ve never lost a game of rock-paper-scissors in under five minutes.

Ventura is the perfect place for someone who loves the ocean but also enjoys the occasional nap on a park bench while watching seagulls argue. I’m ready to contribute to your team with enthusiasm, occasional dad jokes, and a genuine appreciation for the sunset over the pier.

Thank you for considering me. If you need references, I can provide three: my barista, my cat, and the guy who sells me smoothies every Tuesday.

Warmly,  
A Future Ventura Resident"
